_meta.draft_safety_note: This is a Git-first workflow definition. It is not yet executable n8n JSON. It describes the intended logic, contracts, and node structure so that a future n8n implementation can be built safely. All Shopify API calls are read-only. No store data is modified. Credentials are stored only in n8n and must never appear in this file or in Git.
_meta.credential_safety_note: Shopify credentials are referenced by a PLACEHOLDER name below. The actual n8n credential name must be confirmed at runtime. Do not hardcode credential names or values in this file.

workflow.name: import-shopify-data
workflow.purpose: Pull operational store data from Shopify using the active store connection. This workflow is triggered by the master orchestrator after intake-store-input completes. It produces structured raw datasets (shop basics, products, collections, media references) for downstream use in build-store-profile and later planning workflows. All calls are read-only. No data is modified in Shopify.

from_upstream.note: Identifies which Shopify credential/connection to use. Resolves to a specific n8n Shopify credential at runtime. Must never be stored as a domain string in Git artifacts.
Used to tag the imported datasets with the correct project context.

credential_placeholder.name: SHOPIFY_CREDENTIAL_PLACEHOLDER
credential_placeholder.note: Stored in n8n credential vault. Never exposed in Git. The actual credential name must be confirmed during n8n implementation. This workflow assumes a Shopify connection is already configured and active in n8n.

raw_shop_data.description: Basic shop-level metadata fetched from the Shopify Admin API.

raw_products.description: Full product catalog fetched from Shopify. Paginated. All pages merged into a single array.

structure_per_item.title: string
structure_per_item.tags: string[] — comma-separated tags split into array
structure_per_item.metafields_global_title_tag: string | null — SEO title if set
structure_per_item.metafields_global_description_tag: string | null — SEO description if set

raw_products.note: Only products with status=active should be included by default. draft and archived products may be included as a separate dataset if needed for planning.

raw_collections.description: All collections (both custom and smart) fetched from Shopify.
raw_collections.note: Both custom_collections and smart_collections are fetched and merged. Each item is tagged with its type field.

raw_shop_metadata.description: Existing store-level SEO and configuration metadata.

structure.metafields: array of store-level metafield objects where namespace/key is relevant to SEO or store config

existing_seo_fields.title: string | null — global store SEO title if set
existing_seo_fields.description: string | null — global store SEO description if set

raw_shop_metadata.note: Store-level metafields only. Product and collection metafields are handled within their respective datasets.

import_summary.description: Summary of what was imported — counts, timestamps, any errors or skipped items.

structure.skipped_product_count: integer — draft or archived products not included in main dataset

downstream_consumers.note: Out of scope for Phase 1. Documented for future reference.
Out of scope for Phase 1. Documented for future reference.
Out of scope for Phase 1. Documented for future reference.

store-profile.schema.json.note: store-profile.schema.json does not define a raw import schema. This workflow's output feeds build-store-profile, which is responsible for schema validation.

nodes.name: Trigger
nodes.description: Receives the call from the master orchestrator. Accepts shopify_store_selector and project_id as inputs. This workflow is not a standalone trigger — it is always called as a subworkflow.
nodes.output: resolved inputs available in workflow context

nodes.name: Fetch Shop Basics
nodes.type: Shopify node or HTTP Request node
nodes.description: Call Shopify Admin API to retrieve top-level shop information. Uses the Shopify credential resolved from shopify_store_selector. Returns shop_domain, shop_name, shop_currency, shop_plan_name, shop_locale, shop_country_code.

nodes.name: Fetch Products (Paginated)
nodes.type: HTTP Request node with pagination loop or Shopify node
nodes.description: Call Shopify products endpoint with limit=250 per page. Handle pagination by following the Link header for subsequent pages until all products are retrieved. Merge all pages into a single array. Include fields: id, title, handle, body_html, vendor, product_type, tags, status, variants, images, metafields_global_title_tag, metafields_global_description_tag.
nodes.pagination_note: Shopify paginates at 250 items per page. Use cursor-based pagination via Link header (page_info parameter). Loop until no next page is returned.
nodes.rate_limit_note: Shopify REST API has a leaky-bucket rate limit (40 calls/second for standard plans). Add delay between paginated calls if the catalog is large (>500 products).

nodes.name: Fetch Custom Collections (Paginated)
nodes.type: HTTP Request node with pagination loop or Shopify node
nodes.description: Call Shopify custom_collections endpoint with limit=250 per page. Handle pagination. Tag each result with type='custom'.

nodes.name: Fetch Smart Collections (Paginated)
nodes.type: HTTP Request node with pagination loop or Shopify node
nodes.description: Call Shopify smart_collections endpoint with limit=250 per page. Handle pagination. Tag each result with type='smart'.

nodes.name: Merge Collections
nodes.description: Merge custom_collections and smart_collections arrays into a single raw_collections array. Preserve the type field on each item so downstream workflows can distinguish collection types.

nodes.name: Fetch Shop-Level Metafields
nodes.description: Fetch store-level metafields to capture any existing SEO title, description, or store configuration metafields.

nodes.name: Build Import Summary
nodes.description: Compute import_summary: count total products, active products, skipped (non-active) products, total collections. Record the imported_at timestamp, project_id, and shop_domain. Capture any errors encountered during import steps. This summary is used by build-store-profile for catalog_type derivation.

nodes.name: Persist Import Datasets (Optional Intermediate Artifacts)
nodes.type: GitHub node or Write File node
nodes.description: Optionally write the imported datasets to the project folder as intermediate artifacts. This supports the Git-first principle and provides an audit trail. Files written: projects/{project_id}/data/shopify-shop.json, projects/{project_id}/data/shopify-products.json, projects/{project_id}/data/shopify-collections.json, projects/{project_id}/data/shopify-metadata.json, projects/{project_id}/data/import-summary.json. This step is optional at MVP — the datasets can be passed directly in memory if the file write is deferred.

nodes.name: Output to Downstream
nodes.description: Pass all raw datasets (raw_shop_data, raw_products, raw_collections, raw_shop_metadata, import_summary) to the calling orchestrator for routing to build-store-profile and later workflows.

assumptions: A Shopify API credential is already configured in n8n and is active. The actual credential name is unknown at Git-definition time and must be confirmed during n8n implementation.

The shopify_store_selector from the intake resolves to a specific Shopify credential at runtime. The mechanism for this resolution depends on n8n's credential selection approach (e.g., expression-based credential selection or a fixed credential per n8n environment).
Shopify API version 2024-01 is used. This should be updated to the latest stable version at implementation time.

Only products with status=active are included in the main raw_products dataset. Draft and archived products are counted in import_summary but excluded from the primary dataset unless a specific planning need requires them.

Product metafields beyond metafields_global_title_tag and metafields_global_description_tag are not fetched in this phase. Custom metafield namespaces require a separate metafields fetch per product, which adds significant API call volume and is deferred to a later phase.
The import is read-only. No Shopify data is created, modified, or deleted by this workflow.
Large catalogs (>500 products) may require additional rate limit handling. This is documented but not implemented in the draft.
Media asset files (CDN URLs) are captured as part of product images and collection images. Downloading or processing those files is out of scope for this workflow.

open_questions.question: What is the actual n8n credential name for the Shopify connection?
open_questions.impact: Required to configure the credential field in all Shopify/HTTP nodes at implementation time.
open_questions.resolution: Must be confirmed by inspecting the n8n credential vault during implementation. Use a safe placeholder in this definition.

open_questions.question: Does n8n's Shopify node support cursor-based pagination automatically, or must pagination be implemented manually with a loop?
open_questions.impact: Determines node type for steps 3–5. n8n's built-in Shopify node may handle pagination automatically for some resources.
open_questions.recommended_default: Use HTTP Request node with a manual pagination loop for full control and visibility. Switch to Shopify node if it reliably handles pagination for all needed endpoints.

open_questions.question: Should imported raw datasets be persisted to Git (via GitHub node) or kept in memory only?
open_questions.impact: File persistence aligns with Git-first principles and provides an audit trail, but adds GitHub API calls and repo size. In-memory keeps the workflow faster but leaves no trace.
open_questions.recommended_default: Persist to project folder as intermediate artifacts (step 9). This aligns with repo-rules and n8n-conventions. Defer the GitHub write to a post-import step if performance is a concern.

open_questions.question: How should the shopify_store_selector resolve to a Shopify n8n credential when multiple Shopify stores may be connected?
open_questions.impact: n8n credential selection can be done statically (one credential per environment) or dynamically (expression-based). Dynamic selection requires n8n v1.x+ features.
open_questions.recommended_default: For MVP, use a single configured Shopify credential per n8n environment. Document the multi-store pattern for future expansion.

open_questions.question: Should product metafields (beyond SEO title/description) be fetched in this phase?
open_questions.impact: Custom metafields (e.g., product specs, custom badges) may be useful for later content planning but require N+1 API calls (one per product).
open_questions.recommended_default: Defer custom metafield fetching to the build-product-content phase when the data is actually needed. Keep this import lean.

runtime_dependencies: n8n Execute Workflow Trigger node — receives subworkflow call from master orchestrator
n8n Shopify node OR HTTP Request node — for Shopify API calls
n8n Code node (JavaScript) — for merging and summarizing datasets
n8n GitHub node OR Write Binary File node — optional, for persisting datasets to repo
Shopify Admin API credential — stored in n8n, never in Git
Shopify API version: 2024-01 (confirm latest stable version at implementation time)
Shopify API rate limit: leaky-bucket, 40 calls/sec for standard plans — pagination loops must account for this
